calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion For Order Deeming Unanswered Request For Admissions As Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Nco Portfolio Management, Llc
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Katherine Jones' Motion for order deeming unanswered request for admissions as admitted and for monetary sanctions against NCO Portfolio Management LLC granted:
1. Defendant's request for admissions (set one) against cross-defendant NCO Portfolio Management LLC are deemed admitted;
2. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $540.00, payable within thirty days of notice of entry of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanction And Monetary Sanction Against Pltf Ncr, For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Deposition And Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Jun-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Katherine Jones' Motion For Terminating Sanction And Monetary Sanction Against Plaintiff NCO For Failure To Obey Order Compelling Deposition And Production Of Documents.
1. There being no opposition on file, motion for terminating sanction and monetary sanction against plaintiff NCO for failure to obey order compelling deposition and production of documents is granted. Plaintiff's complaint is stricken, and judgment shall be entered in favor of defendant Katherine Jones.
2. Sanctions shall issue in the sum of $540.00.
Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

Notice And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Nco Portfolio Management, Inc., Person Most Knowledgeable And For Production Of Documents And Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-26-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Katherine Jones' Motion to compel deposition of NCO Portfolio Management, Inc. person most knowledgeable and for production of documents granted.
a. Within ten days of the date of service of this order, plaintiff shall produce its person most knowledgeable for deposition, and shall produce documents for inspection and copying, as set forth in defendant's notice of deposition;
b. The deposition shall be conducted at a place, date and time to be designated by defendant.
2. Request for monetary sanctions granted; sanctions shall issue against plaintiff in the sum of $540.00, payable within 30 days of the date of service of this order. Prevailing party to submit form of order. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-05-2007 Dismissal Or Judgment On File As To All Defendants
